records.ety: [OE., fr. Icel. egg; akin to AS. æg (whence OE. ey), Sw. ägg, Dan. æg, G. & D. ei, and prob. to OSlav. aje, jaje, L. ovum, Gr. 'w,o`n, Ir. ugh, Gael. ubh, and perh. to L. avis bird. Cf. Oval.]

defs: The oval or roundish body laid by domestic poultry and other birds, tortoises, etc. It consists of a yolk, usually surrounded by the “white” or albumen, and inclosed in a shell or strong membrane.
A simple cell, from the development of which the young of animals are formed; ovum; germ cell.
Anything resembling an egg in form.
notes: ☞ Egg is used adjectively, or as the first part of self-explaining compounds; as, egg beater or egg-beater, egg case, egg ladle, egg-shaped, etc.

records.ety: [OE. eggen, Icel. eggja, fr. egg edge. <?/<?/. See Edge.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Egged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Egging (?).]

defs: To urge on; to instigate; to incite<?/
quotes: Adam and Eve he egged to ill.
[She] did egg him on to tell How fair she was.
